document: MOUNT VERNON — St. Anne's Community Food Pantry, which has served the city's south side for two decades, announced Tuesday it will cut its distribution schedule from five days a week to two, citing a 40 percent drop in donations since the spring. 'We simply cannot keep the shelves stocked,' director Maria Torres said. The pantry serves roughly 600 families a month. City officials said they were exploring emergency funding options but offered no timeline.

signals.raw_excerpt: it will cut its distribution schedule from five days a week to two, citing a 40 percent drop in donations since the spring

why: Capacity fell by a stated, countable amount (5 days to 2 = magnitude 3 days). Verbatim span justifies it alone. Direction -1: supply falling. The donation drop is context inside the same span, not a second signal.
